definition: Final eligibility based on oncologic context radiological imaging and whether AI imaging was the principal object of evaluation
Whether AI imaging was an explicit primary or major objective intervention comparator or prespecified evaluation
Record-specific rationale supporting final eligibility and AI-focus classification
Whether the registered study was interventional or an observational study with prospective time perspective
Whether a fixed AI imaging tool or workflow was prospectively applied to newly accrued cases
Whether the registered allocation field indicated randomization
Whether allocation changed exposure to AI-assisted imaging or workflow versus standard or non-AI care
Single principal AI-specific evaluation level assigned from registered primary outcomes and the explicit main AI objective
Whether the registered primary AI evaluation concerned patient health or a patient-reported consequence of AI use
Whether the primary level was reader workflow decision patient or economic evaluation
Whether the primary level was clinical decision impact true patient outcome or health-economic evaluation
Whether radiomics was the only recorded AI-method category
Whether radiology reports were the only recorded imaging source
Whether ClinicalTrials.gov indicated posted results at extraction
Whether a completed study had an empirical AI-results PubMed article linked by exact NCT identifier by extraction
Calendar year derived from first-submitted date
Prespecified descriptive submission period
Semicolon-delimited non-mutually-exclusive cancer categories
Semicolon-delimited non-mutually-exclusive imaging modalities or imaging-related sources
Semicolon-delimited non-mutually-exclusive AI-method labels
Semicolon-delimited clinical or technical imaging tasks
Classification from registered facility count
Date of locked registry snapshot
